home.statusCopy: One surface for judgment, rhythm, and alignment.
home.h1:
Protect the standard.
Advance only what earns the name.
home.sub: This room exists to keep Moscatelli coherent: image, material, ritual, people, and launch decisions held within one controlled surface.

mandate.title: Mandate
mandate.h3: An ultra-luxury Roman house built on restraint, authorship, and ritual.
mandate.p: Launch must feel institutional, never promotional. The first task is not breadth but authority: a small capsule, exact packaging, disciplined imagery, and decisions strong enough to survive scrutiny.

cardA.title: Launch priority
cardA.p: Scarf capsule first: Bianco Avorio and Terra Bruna, ivory rigid box, oxblood ritual kept discreet.

cardB.title: Decision rule
cardB.p: Nothing advances without physical validation, founder sign-off, and evidence worth archiving.

cardC.title: Growth discipline
cardC.p: Proof of sales before outside capital. Scale follows traction; it never substitutes for it.

atlas.title: Atlas

card1.title: Scarf / Bianco Avorio
card1.p: Calm exterior. Warm-stone light. Controlled fringe.

card2.title: Scarf / Terra Bruna
card2.p: The darker counterweight: earth, depth, permanence.

card3.title: Packaging / Bianco Avorio
card3.p: Institutional calm. Blind presence. No loud signature.

card4.title: Ritual / Oxblood reserve
card4.p: Oxblood appears as hidden gravity: wax, fold, and interior moment.

atlas.sideP: Keep surfaces matte, shadowed, and quiet. Ivory should feel like warm stone, never bridal white. Oxblood must lean brown, never bright. Every image should imply authority before explanation.

row1.title: Launch scarf
row1.sub: 190 × 31 cm, baby alpaca, controlled drape, straight dense fringe.

row2.title: Packaging doctrine
row2.sub: Ivory rigid box; oxblood reserved for wax seal, envelope, and hidden ritual.

row3.title: Colour approval
row3.sub: Physical lab dips under D65 or agreed equivalent. No screen approvals.

row4.title: Investor rule
row4.sub: Angel capital only after proof of sales, even at modest initial volume.

row5.title: Validation authority
row5.sub: Final aesthetic approval remains with Gianluca alone.

manu.title: Manufattura
manu.quote:
Quiet surfaces.
Exact edges.
Emotional authority at close range.
manu.sub: Current workstreams: scarf sampling, label language, box proportions, launch imagery, and the invisible details that make the house feel inevitable.

metric1.title: Material
metric1.copy: Baby alpaca with a compact matte surface and disciplined fall.

metric2.title: Validation
metric2.copy: Founder-only final sign-off, archived evidence, no casual approvals.

metric3.title: Ritual
metric3.copy: Ivory calm outside; oxblood gravity reserved for the interior moment.

manu.figureP: Soft interior label logic. Quiet typography. “Made in Italy” held with dignity, never noise.

circuit.title: Circuit
circuit.invite: Keep the circle small. Keep the standard absolute.
circuit.note: Rome as house gravity. Brazil as working extension. Prato first, Biella second. Proof before capital.

card1.name: Gianluca
card1.p: Final validation, institutional tone, and the non-negotiable standard.

card2.name: Gabriela
card2.p: Narrative discipline, market framing, and commercial alignment.

card3.name: Marcella
card3.p: Task flow, follow-through, operational rhythm, and continuity across the team.

card4.name: Italian network
card4.p: Prato mills, Biella intelligence, rigid-box partners, and the physical truth of materials on the ground.

atlas1.title: Scarf / Bianco Avorio
atlas1.body:
Bianco Avorio is not white. It must read as calm stone warmed by light — dry, noble, and unforced.

For the scarf, this colour carries the exterior discipline of the house: silence first, softness second. Fringe must remain straight, dense, and governed.

The objective is composure, not softness for its own sake.

atlas2.title: Scarf / Terra Bruna
atlas2.body:
Terra Bruna exists as the darker counterpart to Bianco Avorio: aged wood, raw cacao, earth after rain.

It should never read as generic brown. The role of this colour is to give the launch pair seriousness, depth, and masculine gravity without aggression.

Every photographic use should preserve shadow detail and avoid red contamination.

atlas3.title: Packaging / Bianco Avorio
atlas3.body:
The rigid box must feel institutional, not decorative. Blind embossing is preferable to noisy contrast.

Ivory here works as the architectural face of the house: calm, measured, and almost administrative in its restraint.

The correct sensation is: this object already belongs in an archive.

atlas4.title: Ritual / Oxblood reserve
atlas4.body:
Oxblood is not for public shouting. It belongs to sealed moments: wax, envelope interior, hidden fold, sacred punctuation.

Its role is ceremonial gravity. It should lean brown, matte, and antique — never lipstick red, never glossy, never festive.

Used correctly, it deepens the ritual without cheapening the exterior calm.

codex1.title: Launch scarf
codex1.body:
The launch object is deliberately narrow in scope: one scarf format, one controlled proportion, one material discipline.

Current fixed points: 190 × 31 cm, baby alpaca, compact matte hand, controlled drape, and straight dense fringe.

The point is not novelty. The point is to establish authority through proportion, surface, and finish.

codex2.title: Packaging doctrine
codex2.body:
Primary packaging remains Bianco Avorio. Oxblood is withheld for the inner ritual: wax seal, envelope logic, and hidden moments of gravity.

This separation matters. Exterior calm builds institutional trust; interior oxblood delivers memorability without theatrical excess.

Any packaging proposal that reverses this hierarchy should be rejected.

codex3.title: Colour approval
codex3.body:
Colour is approved physically, not digitally. Lab dips must be read under D65 or an agreed neutral equivalent.

Screen approvals are insufficient because the house standard depends on material truth, not simulated colour.

Approved swatches must be archived with date, lot reference, and founder validation.

codex4.title: Investor rule
codex4.body:
Outside capital enters only after proof of sales. Even modest proof is more valuable than a large speculative story.

This keeps leverage with the house and prevents narrative inflation before the object has earned its place.

The order is fixed: object, response, proof, then scale.

codex5.title: Validation authority
codex5.body:
Final aesthetic approval remains with Gianluca alone. This is not vanity; it is a control mechanism for coherence.

Contributors may recommend, refine, and challenge. They do not close the matter.

What enters the archive must reflect one final house standard, not a negotiated average.

decision1.title: Ivory packaging confirmed as primary direction
decision1.body:
The primary box direction remains Bianco Avorio. The exterior must communicate institutional calm before any decorative emotion appears.

Rationale: ivory supports restraint, permanence, and quiet authority. It gives Moscatelli a controlled first impression rather than a theatrical one.

Decision state: locked, subject only to physical box-sample validation of material, proportion, and finish.

decision2.title: Oxblood reserved as accent and ritual colour
decision2.body:
Rosso Essenza / oxblood remains reserved for the interior ritual: wax seal, envelope logic, hidden detail, or controlled accent.

Rationale: oxblood is powerful when it appears as discovery. Used as the primary box colour, it becomes too heavy and too theatrical for the first expression of the house.

decision3.title: Lotto I remains the proof event
decision3.body:
The first scarf launch exists to prove discipline, commercial response, and taste. It is not a vanity launch and should not be inflated before the object earns confidence.

Rule: object, response, proof, then scale.

decision4.title: Fringe length remains pending physical validation
decision4.body:
The desired direction remains short, dense, disciplined, and straight. The final length must be confirmed by physical sample rather than screen judgement.

Open question: whether the most controlled expression is 3.5 cm or a slightly longer tested alternative.

decision5.title: GSM remains pending sample validation
decision5.body:
The intended hand is compact, refined, matte, and architectural. GSM remains pending because weight alone cannot prove elegance.

Validation requirement: hand feel, drape, surface compactness, warmth, and resistance to blanket-like limpness.

decision6.title: Supplier approach: Prato first, Biella second
decision6.body:
The first reconnaissance direction remains Prato before Biella. This gives Moscatelli a practical route into production conversations before escalating into more selective supplier territory.

Purpose: compare seriousness, MOQ flexibility, sample willingness, material understanding, and credibility.

rejectedArchive.title: Rejected decision archive
rejectedArchive.body:
Oxblood primary box
Rejected because it makes the first physical impression too heavy, too theatrical, and less institutionally calm than ivory.

Ornate hemstitch on first scarf
Rejected for Lotto I because it introduces decorative noise before the product has proven proportion, material, and surface discipline.

Introductory discounting
Rejected because it weakens luxury price authority and trains early clients to expect commercial concession.

Pattern-led first scarf
Rejected for the first expression because the launch must prove restraint, colour, finish, and hand before visual complexity.

decisions.title: Decision Register
decisions.intro: A first static register for locked, active, and pending decisions. This surface exists to prevent repeated debates and preserve the reasoning behind each standard.

d1.title: Ivory packaging confirmed as primary direction
d1.rationale: Primary exterior calm remains Bianco Avorio.

d2.title: Oxblood reserved as accent and ritual colour
d2.rationale: Used for wax, envelope logic, and interior gravity — not the box body.

d3.title: Lotto I scarf launch remains the proof event
d3.rationale: The launch must prove taste, discipline, and sales response before scale.

d4.title: Fringe length remains pending physical validation
d4.rationale: Target discipline exists; final length waits for sample evidence.

d5.title: GSM remains pending sample validation
d5.rationale: The target range must be proven by hand, drape, compactness, and surface.

d6.title: Supplier approach: Prato first, Biella second
d6.rationale: A practical reconnaissance route before higher-commitment supplier conversations.

rejected.intro: Rejected ideas are kept separate so the house remembers what was refused without polluting the active register.

r2.title: Ornate hemstitch on first scarf
r2.reason: Rejected for decorative noise in Lotto I.

lotto.title: Lotto (I - II - III)
lotto.intro: A first static workspace for the proof object. Lotto I is active; Lotto II and III remain reserved until the first product earns expansion.

product.title: Lotto I Scarf
product.copy: The first Moscatelli proof object: narrow, disciplined, materially judged, and not allowed to expand before physical validation.

packaging.copy: Ivory primary box; oxblood accent ritual. Final box dimensions wait for real folding tests with the approved scarf sample.

sample.copy: No final approval by screen, theory, or supplier promise. The physical sample must prove hand, drape, colour, edge, fringe, and fold.

questions.copy: These remain visible so the product does not drift into vague enthusiasm before evidence exists.
questions.q3: Exact box dimensions after folding test

supplierDesk.buttonCopy: Open contacted mills, pending replies, risks, and future supplier structure.
supplierDesk.title: Supplier Desk
supplierDesk.intro: A reserved workspace for mills, packaging, labels, trims, and production support. Current mill outreach is logged here without treating any supplier as approved.

placeholders.copy: Use this structure only for additional verified or contacted suppliers. Do not invent supplier records.
placeholders.name: Supplier name

example.title: Supplier entry template
example.note: Template row only; replace later with verified supplier data.

risks.copy: A compact register of issues that can damage Lotto I before production even begins.
risks.r1: MOQ too high for proof-stage production
risks.r2: Prototype pricing too expensive for the launch budget
risks.r3: Finish too soft, fuzzy, or blanket-like
risks.r4: Supplier unwilling to support a low initial quantity
risks.r5: Packaging dimensions unresolved before sample folding tests

details.template: This row exists only as a structural example. Do not treat it as a real supplier record.
details.empty: Reserved position for future verified suppliers. No invented company names should be placed here.
details.research: Research status means no commitment, no contact, and no evidence yet.

contacted.nextAction: Await reply; follow up after 5–7 working days if silent.
contacted.note: Initial scarf sample outreach sent. Await capability, sample cost, lead time, MOQ per colour, indicative unit price, and yarn-sourcing answer.

manifatturabig.name: Manifattura BIG
manifatturabig.nextAction: No Lotto I action; retain only as a future higher-volume or heavier-article contact.
manifatturabig.note: Final assessment: unsuitable for Lotto I. Their response points to articles outside the required construction/spec direction, yarn/title and finished-weight direction too heavy for the target, and MOQ at 300 pcs/colour. Keep warm only for future scale or heavier articles.

sasso.name: Sciarpificio dei Fratelli Sasso / Artknit route
sasso.nextAction: Await Artknit reply or direct Sasso introduction; do not treat as factory-confirmed yet.
sasso.note: Biella scarf-maker lead currently approached through Artknit. Capability must still be verified directly before any sample route is trusted.

gammatex.name: Gammatex
gammatex.nextAction: Await reply; verify whether their alpaca and scarf capability can meet Lotto I discipline.
gammatex.note: Second-wave outreach sent. Public fit is plausible for scarves, stoles and alpaca, but Lotto I requires direct confirmation of fibre, surface, GSM, fringe and MOQ.

kaleidos.name: Kaleidos
kaleidos.nextAction: Research correct contact route before outreach.
kaleidos.note: Held as a second-wave lead only. Do not advance to contacted status until the correct route is confirmed and the first email is actually sent.

responded.nextAction: No Lotto I action; retain as future wool/cashmere contact only.
responded.note: Final reply: no matching article and no routine use of the requested baby alpaca yarn. From-zero development would require testing finishes, yields, yarn sourcing, and several hundred pieces as sampling. Thread closed respectfully; not active for Lotto I.

archived.nextAction: No Lotto I action; retain as future wool/cashmere contact only.
archived.note: Final reply: no matching article and no routine use of the requested baby alpaca yarn. From-zero development would require testing finishes, yields, yarn sourcing, and several hundred pieces as sampling. Thread closed respectfully; not active for Lotto I.

dashboardDetails.lotto: The first static Lotto I workspace is now present: dimensions, material direction, GSM, fringe, colourways, packaging dependency, and open technical questions.
dashboardDetails.decisions: Decision Register is now active: locked, pending, active, and rejected entries are held as the first founder-validation ledger.
dashboardDetails.suppliers: Supplier Desk now records seven mill records: four active contact routes pending reply, MA.AL.BI. and Manifattura BIG archived for Lotto I, and Kaleidos queued for second-wave research. The Sasso route remains indirect through Artknit until factory-confirmed.
dashboardDetails.budget: Reserved for Finance Suite sync or manual KPI entry: budget, committed spend, remaining runway, and unit-economics warnings.
dashboardDetails.launch: Launch Readiness is now embedded inside Dashboard as a compact checklist for product, production, packaging, website, finance, fulfilment, investor proof, and admin readiness.
dashboardDetails.locked: Reserved for the latest founder-approved decision with date, reasoning, and supporting evidence.

statusDetails.launchProgressProduct: In progress means the area exists in Studio, but proof, evidence, or final approval remains incomplete.
statusDetails.launchProgressSuppliers: In progress means supplier outreach is now broader, but no mill is approved until capability, sample cost, lead time, MOQ, yarn availability, fringe discipline, and physical sample quality are confirmed.
statusDetails.launchPendingValidation: Pending validation means the direction is accepted, but the physical test has not yet confirmed dimensions, fit, or finish.
statusDetails.launchPendingStructure: Pending structure means the area must be mapped before it can be judged ready or confirmed.
statusDetails.launchNotStarted: Not started means no approved evidence, schedule, or deliverable has been recorded inside Studio yet.
statusDetails.launchPendingSync: Pending synchronization means the external tool or source exists, but Dashboard does not yet surface its current truth.
statusDetails.launchPendingEvidence: Pending evidence means the claim must wait for product, sale, document, or validation proof.
statusDetails.launchPendingReview: Pending review means the area has not yet been audited enough to receive a serious status.
statusDetails.lottoDimensions: Working direction: keep this proportion unless mill standardisation improves consistency without weakening the object.
statusDetails.lottoMaterial: Material direction is confirmed for the first proof object unless sampling exposes a serious quality or sourcing issue.
statusDetails.lottoYarn: Technical range to discuss with mills; final yarn choice must be proven by sample hand, drape, and surface compactness.
statusDetails.lottoGsm: Weight cannot be approved by number alone; final GSM must survive touch, drape, compactness, warmth, and anti-blanket discipline.
statusDetails.lottoFringe: Target remains short and controlled; final approval waits for physical tests of density, splay, parallel fall, and edge discipline.
statusDetails.lottoFinish: The surface must feel refined and architectural; excessive fuzz, blanket softness, or limp drape should trigger rejection.
statusDetails.lottoTerra: Deep warm chocolate / mahogany-adjacent brown. Must avoid generic flat brown or visible red contamination.
statusDetails.lottoAvorio: Warm ivory, not optical white, not bridal, not yellowed. Must read as calm stone warmed by light.
statusDetails.lottoPackaging: Box dimensions should not be confirmed until the sample is folded, tested for compression, and photographed in the intended ritual sequence.
statusDetails.lottoSample: Validation requires physical review and evidence worthy of archiving before any production commitment.
statusDetails.supplierTemplate: This row exists only as a structural example. Do not treat it as a real supplier record.
statusDetails.supplierResearch: Research status means no commitment, no contact, and no evidence yet.
statusDetails.supplierContacted: Initial email sent. No supplier is approved until capability, sample cost, lead time, MOQ, yarn availability, fringe discipline, and physical sample quality are confirmed.
statusDetails.supplierResponded: MA.AL.BI. confirmed that they do not currently hold this type of baby alpaca article or yarn; a from-zero development would require testing finishes, yields, yarn sourcing, and several hundred pieces as sampling. Archived for Lotto I, retained only for future wool/cashmere possibilities.
statusDetails.supplierArchived: MA.AL.BI. confirmed that they do not currently hold this type of baby alpaca article or yarn; a from-zero development would require testing finishes, yields, yarn sourcing, and several hundred pieces as sampling. Archived for Lotto I, retained only for future wool/cashmere possibilities.
statusDetails.supplierIndirect: Outreach has gone through an intermediary route. Treat as pending intelligence, not direct factory confirmation, until the actual mill or a reliable representative answers with technical capability.
statusDetails.supplierQueued: Queued means the lead is recognised but not yet contacted. No assumptions should be made about capability, MOQ, pricing, sample route, or willingness to work with Moscatelli.
statusDetails.supplierManifatturaBigArchived: Manifattura BIG is archived for Lotto I: the article construction/spec direction is not aligned, the yarn/title and finished-weight direction sit too heavy for the target Nm 2/40–2/48, ~205–215 g/m² scarf, and MOQ is 300 pcs/colour. Retain only as a future scale or heavier-article contact.
